checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill end=2011-12-31 value=61144000; Goodwill end=2012-12-31 value=175108000
OtherAssetsNoncurrent end=2011-12-31 value=8248000; OtherAssetsNoncurrent end=2012-12-31 value=11034000

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet end=2011-12-31 value=69911000; InventoryNet end=2012-12-31 value=84599000 (21.0% growth)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2010-12-31 value=37362000; end=2011-12-31 value=40550000 (AR grew 8.5% while allowance fell 21.8%)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2012-12-31 value=892000 (recovered vs AR end=2012-12-31 value=53402000)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filing_date=2013-03-01 filed on normal schedule; no NT 10-K or NT 10-Q present in chronology

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided for SalesRevenueNet, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingCashFlow, InventoryNet, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent spanning 2009-01-01 through 2013-03-31
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill only reported through end=2011-12-31 value=21254000, with no 2012 data point provided
evidence.location: 10-K FY2011 balance sheet (data gap noted for 2012)

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew 31.7% in FY2012 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent 40,550,000 -> 53,402,000) far outpacing 9.7% revenue growth (SalesRevenueNet 359,449,000 -> 394,288,000), though this coincides with a large Q4 2012 acquisition (Goodwill jumped from 61,144,000 to 175,108,000 and LongTermDebt from 30,737,000 to 237,566,000) that plausibly explains much of the divergence via purchase-accounting consolidation rather than organic channel-stuffing.

Inventory grew faster than revenue in 2012 (InventoryNet +21.0% vs revenue +9.7%) while gross margin remained stable/improving (46.0%->46.2%), consistent with acquisition-related inventory build rather than margin manipulation.
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined 21.8% in FY2011 (593,000->464,000) while receivables grew 8.5%, a mild reserve-adequacy flag that reversed by FY2012 (892,000 allowance).

Overall earnings quality indicators are favorable: operating cash flow exceeded net income in every period examined (e.g., 2012 OCF 46,938,000 vs NI 19,710,000), and quarterly earnings show genuine volatility (including a loss quarter in 2010 and a sharp Q4 2012 NI drop to 641,000), arguing against income smoothing or aggressive accrual management.

Filing chronology shows normal 10-K/10-Q cadence with no late-filing (NT) notices; clustered 8-K/A amendments in late 2012/early 2013 are consistent with routine acquisition pro forma disclosure (Item 9.01) rather than restatement irregularities.
